title: Chapter 6: Preparation
content: Marcus had never prepared for anything in his life.

School presentations? Winged it. Job interviews? Bullshitted it. Family dinners? Avoided it. His entire existence prior to the apocalypse had been a masterclass in reactive living—responding to whatever the world threw at him with the minimum effort required to keep existing.
But this was different. This was a quest with a timer, a woman in danger, and a fortress on the line. This was a raid, and raids required prep.
"System, what do I have that's impressive?"

**[Current Inventory:]**
**[- 2x Canned Meals (Beef Stew)]**
**[- 1x Bottled Water]**
**[- 1x Crowbar]**
**[- Steel-Reinforced Door (installed)]**
"Great. I'm going to impress a traumatized woman and intimidate a sociopath with canned stew."

**[Additional Assets:]**
**[- 1x Stage 2 Zombie Queen (loyal, lethal, very pretty)]**
**[- 1x Reinforced Apartment (fortified)]**
**[- 1x Romance System (you're welcome)]**
"I can't exactly parade Sera around like a—actually, wait."

He looked at Sera. She was sitting at his kitchen table—his kitchen table, like she belonged there—sorting through a pile of scavenged supplies she'd gathered from the hallway while he'd been strategizing. A pair of scissors. A mostly-intact first aid kit from a neighbor's fallen bag. Three batteries. A hair tie.
"You were a CEO," he said.
"I was."
"How did you intimidate people?"

Sera looked up from the batteries. Her expression shifted—the ghost of a predatory smile playing at the corners of her lips. "I didn't intimidate them. I made them want to impress *me*. There's a difference. Intimidation is about fear. Influence is about desire. You don't make someone fear you have something better. You make them *want* it."
"So I need to make Derek want what I have?"

"No. You need to make Elena want what you have. Derek will react to her wanting it. His control depends on her having no better options. Remove that, and he has nothing." Sera leaned back. "In business, we called it 'leveraging the decision-maker.' In a hostage situation, it's called 'giving the hostage a door.'"
She paused, and something darker crossed her face. "He's going to get violent when he feels that control slipping. Are you prepared for that?"
"No."
"Honest."
"It's literally the first time anyone's asked me to be brave for someone else. I'm a gamer, not a hero."

"Gamers fight bosses, don't they? This is just a boss fight with worse graphics and no checkpoint." Sera stood. "But you need better equipment. And you need a plan that doesn't involve me ripping his throat out, because Elena won't trust you if she thinks you're just trading one monster for another."

**[SUB-QUEST: Gear Up]**
**[Complete one of the following to unlock equipment for the rescue:]**
**[Option A: Scavenge the 3rd floor — Risk: Moderate (zombie presence)]**
**[Option B: Complete intimacy milestone with Sera — Risk: Variable]**
**[Reward for A: 1x Tactical Flashlight, 1x Emergency Radio]**
**[Reward for B: 1x Premium Feed Item + 1x System Outfit (Companion Enhancement)]**
"Intimacy milestone?" Marcus felt heat rise to his cheeks. "What does that even—"

**[Intimacy Milestones are romantic interactions that deepen your bond with Seraphina and unlock companion-specific enhancements.]**
**[Available Milestone: Hand-Holding Session (10 minutes, continuous)]**
**[Current Affection: 39/100 — Threshold: 40 to unlock Milestone rewards]**
**[You are 1 point away. Sincere emotional engagement will bridge the gap.]**

"You need one more point," Sera said, reading the System text over his shoulder—or however she perceived it; she'd described the System as "a faint golden glow around your head." "And then we hold hands for ten minutes."
"That's it? Ten minutes of hand-holding?"
"Ten minutes of *continuous* hand-holding while maintaining eye contact," Sera clarified. "The System doesn't make it easy."
She extended her hand across the table.

Her fingers were still cold. Always cold. But the cold didn't shock him anymore—it was becoming a texture he associated with safety, with the woman who'd killed five zombies in seven seconds to protect him.
He took her hand.
Her pupils dilated. The grey irises sharpened.

**[Intimacy Milestone Initiated: Hand-Holding Session]**
**[Duration Required: 10:00]**
**[Condition: Continuous skin contact, maintain proximity]**
**[Timer: 09:59]**
Nine minutes and fifty-nine seconds. Marcus could handle nine minutes and fifty-nine seconds. He'd sat through longer loading screens.
Except—

Sera's thumb traced a slow circle on the back of his hand. Her other hand came up to rest on his forearm, fingers cool against his pulse point. She leaned forward, reducing the distance between them to inches.
"You're nervous," she observed.
"Obviously."
"Why? It's just hand-holding."
"It's hand-holding with a woman who could crush my skull like a grape."
"I would never crush your skull." A pause. "Unless you asked nicely."
"That's not reassuring!"

"It wasn't meant to be." The smile again—that dangerous, beautiful smile that made his heart do complicated things. "But honestly, Marcus—you're the first person in my entire existence, alive or dead, who held my hand because you *wanted* to, not because you were taking something from me."
The words landed like a stone in still water. Ripples spread through his chest.
"What do you mean?"

"I mean that in my old life, every touch had a transaction. Boardroom handshakes were power plays. My mother kissed my cheek at galas because it looked good for the cameras. Men touched my waist at parties because they thought my net worth made me accessible." Her voice was quiet, precise—the voice of someone used to turning pain into data. "You hold my hand because it makes me feel less alone. That's... new."

**[Affection: 39 → 45/100]**
**[Milestone threshold exceeded. Bonus: +4 from genuine emotional disclosure]**

**[MILESTONE COMPLETE: Hand-Holding Session]**
**[Duration: 10:00 / 10:00]**
**[Rewards:]**
**[- 1x Premium Feed Item: Prime Spirit Meat]**
**[- 1x System Outfit: Shadow Weave (Companion Enhancement)]**
**[- Affection Gate Passed: Stage 2→3 Evolution now possible at Affection 50+]**

The Prime Spirit Meat materialized on the table—a cut of meat so perfect it looked CGI, marbled with veins of something iridescent that caught the dim light. The Shadow Weave outfit appeared draped over a chair: a sleek black ensemble that looked like tactical wear designed by a fashion house—form-fitting jacket, reinforced pants, boots that somehow looked both elegant and combat-ready.
Sera stood and touched the fabric. Her expression shifted—not quite surprise, but something close to wonder.
"It feels... warm," she said. "Like wearing sunlight."
**[Shadow Weave: +20% movement speed, +15% damage resistance, aesthetic enhancement (subjective)]**
"It looks good on you," Marcus said, then immediately wanted to swallow the words. Too forward. Too—
"Thank you." Sera pulled on the jacket, rolled her shoulders, moved through a few experimental motions—fast, fluid, almost invisible in the apartment's shadows. "Very good. I'm faster in this."
"Great. Faster zombie queen. Just what I needed."

"Your zombie queen." She turned to face him, and in the low light, with the Shadow Weave hugging her frame and her grey eyes catching the glow of System text, she looked like something out of a game—a premium companion character that players would spend real money to unlock.
"Get some sleep," she said. "Tomorrow, we rescue Elena. Tonight, I'm on watch."
"You don't sleep?"
"Not anymore. One of the benefits of being dead." A beat. "Go. I'll keep you safe."

Marcus went to his bed—the same bed where Sera had evolved, where she'd said his name for the first time, where she'd gripped his arm and refused to let go. The sheets smelled faintly of cold flowers and copper.
Tomorrow, he'd face Derek. Tomorrow, he'd win Elena. Tomorrow, he'd claim a titanium fortress.
Tonight, he closed his eyes, and for the second time since the world ended, he slept without nightmares.
Because Sera was watching.
And nothing got past Sera.
